rulings.Case Title: GERALD BROOKS,SR. VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons And Defendant Kyle Railways, Inc.S Demurrer To Plaintiff Geral Brooks Complaint And Amendment To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, OCTOBER 22, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT KYLE RAILWAYS, INC'S MOTION To Quash Service Of Summons And Demurrer To Plaintiff Geral Brooks Complaint And Amendment To Complaint. Motion to quash is denied. There is no requirement under section 474 that a plaintiff exercise reasonable diligence in discovering the true identity of fictitious defendants. 184 CA 3rd 376. This is so even if ignorance due to plaintiff's negligence. 183 CA 3rd 352. Defendant must provide evidence that plaintiff was actually aware of defendant's name at the time of filing. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. The statute of limitations applies separately for each asbestos-related disease, 83 CA 4th 1444. Statute of limitations would not run as to asbestosis if that was first diagnosed in December '03. =(302/JKS/xSN)

rulings.Case Title: GARY ALEXANDER et al VS. DAVID J. COOK et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, OCTOBER 22, 2007. LINE 12, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM KEEGAN AND THE KEEGAN LAW FIRM'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CCP SECTION 425.16 APPLIES TO MALICIOUS PROSECUTION ACTIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE SUFFICIENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO PERMIT A FINDING THAT THEY WILL LIKELY PREVAIL ON EACH ELEMENT OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE PRIOR ACTION "WAS INTIATED BY OR AT THE DIRECTION OF THE DEFENDANT" WITH RESPECT TO DEFENDANTS HELLER, EHRMAN, SMITH AND KEEGAN, AND DID NOT PROVIDE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE ACTION "WAS BROUGHT WITHOUT PROBABLE CAUSE" AND "INITIATED WITH MALICE" AS TO ALL DEFENDANTS INCLUDING COOK. (302/JKS/LB)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW GOVAN-SMITH VS. RONALD S GREEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, UNLAWFUL DETAINER
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, OCTOBER 22, 2007, LINE 17. DEFENDANT RONALD GREEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant filed a motion for summary judgment and did not seek summary adjudication. If there is one triable issue of fact on any cause of action, the motion must be denied. 189 CA 3rd 1542, 1546. There are several triable issues of fact, eg., 1- whether defendant repudiated the mediation agreement; 2- whether plaintiff demanded the precise sum due; 3- can a tenant withold rent for breach of quiet enjoyment as opposed to breach of habitability? Was there an actial partial eviction? How much rent should be reduced? =(302/JKS/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA LIN VS. CHRISTOPHER KENNER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, OCTOBER 22, 2007. LINE 18, DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER KENNER AND GEOFFREY GEMMELL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, 5 DAYS TO ANSWER. SINCE THE NOTICE DID NOT STATE THAT PERSONAL PAYMENT WAS ACCEPTABLE, PLAINTIFF DID NOT NEED TO PROVIDE THE INFORMATION RE THE USUAL DAYS AND HOURS OF ACCEPTANCE. PER CCP 1161(2), DEFENDANT COULD HAVE MADE PAYMENT BY MAIL. (302/JKS/PB)
